# JuriCodex

> Source-grounded legal research for U.S. law. Find legal authorities, inspect citations, and verify quoted language.

JuriCodex is a legal research workspace for U.S. law. It helps users search real cases, statutes, and regulations; review citations; verify quoted text; extract citations from pasted legal text; and organize research into a table of authorities.

JuriCodex is a research tool, not legal advice. It does not replace a licensed attorney and should not be used as the sole basis for a legal decision.

## Primary URLs

- Home: https://juricodex.online/
- Research note: https://juricodex.online/blog/legal-research-should-show-sources.html
- Terms: https://juricodex.online/terms.html
- Privacy: https://juricodex.online/privacy.html

## What the product does

- Legal research chat grounded in real legal authorities
- Case, citation, keyword, concept, statute, and regulation search
- Quote verification against source text where available
- Brief review and citation extraction
- Case details, source links, and table-of-authorities style output

## Audience

People who need to find and understand U.S. legal authorities, including pro se litigants, legal aid workers, students, researchers, and small firms.

## Boundaries

- JuriCodex provides legal research assistance, not legal advice.
- Users should verify sources before relying on them.
- Users should consult a licensed attorney for legal decisions.
